learnerValue: Reinforces the precise conservation-of-momentum rule for a closed system.
Links an applied force directly to a change in momentum.
Builds accurate recall of compressions and rarefactions in longitudinal waves.
Distinguishes the three possible outcomes for waves at material boundaries.
Practises the complete electromagnetic-spectrum order while linking wavelength and frequency.
Secures the investigated relationship and variables in required practical activity 10.
Separates the nuclear origin of gamma rays from broader atomic changes and biological effects.
Tests the correct association between microwaves and their specified applications.
Clarifies the important difference between images formed by convex and concave lenses.
Reinforces selective absorption and transmission as the mechanism of colour filters.
Combines all defining absorption, reflection, transmission and emission properties of a perfect black body.
Targets the two emission characteristics controlled by temperature.
Builds a clear comparison between permanent and induced magnetism.
Connects current to magnetic-field production and recalls both field-strength factors.
Reinforces gravity's role as the force maintaining circular orbital motion.
Links the observation of galactic red-shift to expansion and support for the Big Bang theory.
